proof_sketch: The mutated loop condition changes from i < VEC_SIZE to i <= VEC_SIZE, adding an iteration i=4. Each thread with idx < size now processes elements idx..idx+4. The element at idx+4 is also processed by the next thread (starting at idx+4). Both threads write the same hardtanh output to that location. Since the writes are identical, the final output is bitwise unchanged for all input values. The inner if(pos < size) prevents out-of-bounds access. All tests passed because no input can make the two writes differ.

So the last block may have some threads where idx+4 is beyond size, but due to the inner check, they skip.
